channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Torbay Road near Tesco, 2nd April
item.description:
Lots of rubbish on Torbay Road on pavement and street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Willesden Lane, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken Zipline, 2nd April
item.description:
My sister recently went to the Erith Recreation Ground Playground and she informed me that the zipline is broken. The zipline is her favourite part of the playground and I would really appreciate if the zipline could be fixed. Not just for my sister but also for other children to be able to play with.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thanet Road, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is no road, the whole thing is a problem, 2nd April
item.description:
The road is completely messed up, you can’t drive there, the whole road is a big pothole
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Westgate Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 2nd April
item.description:
This vehicle has been left in our parking area for weeks. It is not taxed or MOTd.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Crosslet Vale, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Anerley Hill SE19 within a coupl, 2nd April
item.description:
Anerley Hill SE19 within a couple of yards of streetlight column ID number 4 (close to property number 6) is a square black plastic footpath litter bin which is graffiti tagged. Google Streetview link: https://www.google.com/maps/@51.4197791,-0.0773775,3a,75y,184.13h,84.02t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1scBF-6Ewh0ZgZFeqFnDU4Gw!2e0!6shttps:%2F%2Fstreetviewpixels-pa.googleapis.com%2Fv1%2Fthumbnail%3Fcb_client%3Dmaps_sv.tactile%26w%3D900%26h%3D600%26pitch%3D5.978579544880773%26panoid%3DcBF-6Ewh0ZgZFeqFnDU4Gw%26yaw%3D184.13027369871196!7i16384!8i8192?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI2MDQwMS4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Anerley Hill, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree growing over the back of our garage is causing a lot of damage to mine and other neighbours garages and is also a really unpleasant sight when looking out my rear windows and back doors., 2nd April
item.description:
This tree i can imagine has been here for quite a few years and looks like it desperately needs pruning right back due to the damage it’s causing on the garage roofs

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gattons Way, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Bags of clothes dumped on street corner of McLeod rd and Crumpsall street, SE2.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mcleod Road, Abbey Wood

item.title: Dead fox opposite 254 westmount road, 2nd April
item.description:
We have moved the dead fox from the middle of the road to the pavement opposite 254 westmount road, so not to cause any car accidents. May be distressing for any young children passing in the morning so please can it be removed
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westmount Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 2nd April
item.description:
Fly tipping (a mattress, bedframes, broken wooden furniture and other household items) on our already overfilled communal waste area.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rendlesham Road, Hackney, London

Bin with overflowing waste needs to be emptied.

item.title: Street light, 2nd April
item.description:
The street lights outside my house are not working , it’s dark and dangerous, especially with all the brake ins in the last few months .
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Regeneration Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked driveway, 2nd April
item.description:
Car parked across dropped kerb blocking access to driveway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lockmead Road, Hither Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole with loose road surface, 2nd April
item.description:
This is a new report for the same defect covered by report 9124816, which the Council has closed to further comments after repeatedly dismissing the complaint using identical template responses. The pothole at the Kemnal Road / Ashfield Lane junction has not been fixed and is significantly worse than when first reported on 17 March 2026. The Council's latest response describes the defect as "surface scabbing" below its 30mm investigatory threshold. That assessment is plainly wrong. The defect is at least 50mm deep with sharp broken edges, an uneven and degraded surface, and clear evidence of rapid deterioration. Any vehicle crossing this junction risks tyre and suspension damage, even at low speeds. This is a foreseeable danger to road users at a point where vehicles must slow or turn. The Council has closed report 9124816 four times while the defect has visibly worsened. Either the inspections were not carried out competently, or they were not carried out at all. No reasonable inspector measuring this defect could have concluded that it falls below the 30mm threshold. Under section 41 of the Highways Act 1980, the Council has a statutory duty to maintain this highway. I request a further inspection with the depth properly measured and recorded, written confirmation of the date, time, method, and identity of the inspector for each previous inspection, and prompt remedial works. I am obtaining photographs with measurements for evidential purposes. The history of this report, including the Council's repeated closures without competent investigation despite a known and worsening defect, is directly relevant to the viability of any section 58 defence in the event of a claim.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ashfield Lane, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple cars blocking driveway and on double yellow blops, 2nd April
item.description:
As normal this time of night. I cannot get my car out.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegal parking, 2nd April
item.description:
Cash only mechanic parks cars on yellow lines every day here. They operate behind the graffiti gates

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lamb Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant conjestion on Pomeroy Street, 2nd April
item.description:
I have travelled this road a number of times and in almost each time there has been travel chaos caused by the srtreet being too narrow for two-way traffic combined with parking. Last Tuesday was the worst. There were two large trucks attempting to travel Southwards and a number of drivers had to get out of their cars to direct traffic in order to end the total gridlock. This road needs to be either one-way or parking free.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 37, Pomeroy Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
